name: DryrunVR
title: VR Safety Drill Builder for High-Risk Crews
oneLiner: Self-serve platform that converts a factory's existing written SOPs into immersive VR safety drills — no $50K custom build.

tags.target: Safety managers at 50-500 employee manufacturers, warehouses, contractors

summary.problem: Mid-market industrial companies carry serious workplace-safety liability every shift, but training budgets only cover slideshows and sign-off sheets. Custom VR training starts around $50K per build; generic VR libraries feel like video games built around the wrong equipment. Safety managers choose between compliance theater and enterprise price tags.
summary.solution: Upload a confined-space entry procedure or lockout-tagout protocol; the system parses steps, hazards, and decision points, then assembles VR drills from pre-built asset libraries (machinery, PPE, electrical panels, warehouse environments) that mirror what workers actually walk past on shift. Floor managers author new exercises without code.
summary.wedge: One procedure type (lockout-tagout) for one vertical (mid-market manufacturing) where OSHA citations are common and painful.

sources.title: IdeaBrowser — Idea of the Day email, 2026-06-20 (Factory VR trainer)
